title: Missing annotations added datastructures & requests modules

issues.body: Should this just be `-> typing.AsyncGenerator[bytes]` ?

I think we should probably drop `JSONType` and just use `Any`.
(Since the type system can't deal with the recursive type definition anyways)
Should we drop `"type: ignore" here?
Just `other: typing.Any` here is fine.
`raw_headers` is an (optional) list of two-tuples of `byte`, `byte`.
Can we drop the `type: ignore` here?
Can we drop the `type: ignore` here?
Can we drop the `type: ignore` here?

I guess `default` should be an Optional string here. (Tho just use `default: str = None` for the annotation, since that'll indicate it as optional anyways)

Also the return type of the function will be `typing.Optional[str]`.
I think we ought to be able to drop this once the type changes in the method signature are made.
I think `str` rather than `typing.Any`?
I think `str` rather than `typing.Any`?
Same again - the keys are strings - it oughta indicate incorrect usage if a different type is used as the key, right?
Just `other: typing.Any` is fine here. It's redundant to have a union of Any with another type.
Use `typing.Optional[int]` for these kind of signatures.
We have `Scope`, `Receive` etc. defined in `types.py` I guess import and use those.
I'm happy with just `typing.Any` here, since we can't properly type json anyways.
yes, should be `-> typing.AsyncGenerator[bytes, None]`
yes, cool! I didn't realize that!! Thanks

yes, the key should be a string. The thing is when I set `key: string` I have the following errors:

```
error: Argument 1 of "__contains__" incompatible with supertype "Mapping"
error: Argument 1 of "__contains__" incompatible with supertype "Container"
```

What I understand is that I have

Okay, Ive bumped into that before - that's tedious.

I expect that there may be more correct ways to deal with it by eg. subclassing a generic type, but I'm not sure. Personally I think we should consider just switching off mypy's "incompatible with supertype" errors. We're doing a perfectly corre

Yes, I was expecting the same. I am using `mypy starlette --disallow-untyped-defs`, the same that is described on the #7 

Mostly all errors are related with `incompatible with supertype` on `datastructure.py`. 
I've finished with all the corrections in `requests.py`. 

Yes, after all the annot

I asked about it and whether there is a flag to switching off mypy's " incompatible with supertype" errors on `mypy`. The answer was: 

`There is no global flag for this, because Liskov principle is like one of cornerstones of the type system. You can silence the error by # type: ignore of course.
Okay, sticking with `typing.Any` there make sense 👍
Ok, cool. I will define the type `BytesPairs = typing.List[typing.Tuple[bytes, bytes]]`

We need to define the type, otherwise we have `error: Need type annotation for '_list'`

In this case should be: `self._list = []  # type: BytesPairs`
Here we have the same problem as before if we use `typing.Optional[str]` for `default` (`incompatible with supertype`) . We should use `typing.Any`. On other hand, `key` should be `str` I guess.

We can't. If we remove it we have ` Return type of "items" incompatible with supertype "Mapping"`. Anyway, if the returned value is different from `StrPairs`  `Incompatible return value type` error is raised.

If we drop `type: ignore` we have `Signature of "replace" incompatible with supertype "str"`. I think is a good idea to keep it. If the returned value type is different from `URL`, the error ` Incompatible return value type`is raised.
